title: Cross-fitted CatBoost on raw categorical identities instead of mostly frequency encodings
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality Driver and many race-context combinations; CatBoost’s ordered target statistics were designed specifically to fight prediction shift and target leakage in categorical boosting.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality Driver and many race-context combinations; CatBoost’s ordered target statistics were designed specifically to fight prediction shift and target leakage in categorical boosting. That is a materially different learner from the current mostly manual feature engineering plus category/frequency treatment.
implementation_hint: Run CatBoost with raw Driver/Race/Compound and selected combinations like Driver+Race, Driver+Compound, Race+Compound, but only under strict out-of-fold training and race-grouped validation. Compare plain ordered boosting vs the existing engineered matrix, and also test CatBoost on a reduced feature set to see whether ordered categorical memory beats hand-built frequency features.
expected_effect: May recover signal from sparse driver-race-compound identities and historically similar strategy contexts that frequency encoding flattens away.
risk: If folds are not grouped by race/year, ordered stats can still look better than they should; CatBoost may underperform if numeric interactions already dominate.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
